action.reasoning: Observation: In iteration 28, the blind swerve escape maneuver failed completely (0 safe steps) because it triggered falsely and caused Spot to run away from the human. Analysis: Every attempt to add explicit stateful unstuck/escape logic (orbit, swerve, flee) since iteration 25 has degraded performance. The pure P-controller from iteration 25 achieved the highest score (81.54 safe steps) because it is naturally robust and doesn't get stuck in false-positive recovery loops. The only issue in iteration 25 was early termination when the human got too close while Spot was backing up. Decision: I will completely remove all stateful recovery logic and revert to the pure, stateless P-controller from iteration 25. I will increase `target_dist` slightly to 1.65m to provide a better buffer against early termination, and I will improve the 'human behind Spot' logic to run straight forward without turning, ensuring a clean escape. I will update predict.py and test_follower.py, run test_follower.py to verify, and submit eval.
